title: Obsidian-style Graph View
problem: The tree view shows lineage as an indented list but hides the topology — readers cannot see which features are densely connected, isolated, or form clusters.
analysis: D3 force simulation positions nodes by repulsion + spring forces along parent/child edges. The graph shares the app's existing focusKey/focus state so both views stay in sync. Node size (5–14px) encodes completeness_pct. Two colour modes driven by a colorBy prop: 'status' maps to warm palette constants (STATUS_NODE_COLOR/STATUS_GLOW); 'domain' maps 14 known domains to evenly-spaced HSL hues and adds forceX/Y cluster forces that pull nodes toward pre-computed domain centres on a circle at 28% of canvas radius, with SVG cluster labels. Hover shows a floating tooltip; click pins a full info panel (featureKey, title, status, domain, problem text). Ctrl+click calls onNodeJump to switch to tree view. Clicking also centres the graph on the node via an animated D3 zoom/pan transition. The graph receives filteredFeatures from App so filter state is honoured in graph view.

decisions.decision: Reuse the existing focusKey state rather than a separate graph-selection concept
decisions.rationale: The app already has a working focus mechanism with URL sync and breadcrumb — sharing it means clicking a graph node and switching to tree view highlights the same feature.
alternativesConsidered: Separate selectedKey for graph — would duplicate URL param and breadcrumb logic

decisions.decision: Dark warm background for the graph canvas (#1C1714)
decisions.rationale: The Obsidian graph idiom reads best on dark background — using the --text colour preserves the archival palette rather than reaching for a generic dark.
alternativesConsidered: Light cream background — nodes washed out and edges invisible
Pure black — breaks the warm aesthetic

decisions.decision: View toggle (tree / graph) in the section header, not a separate route
decisions.rationale: The app has no router — a toggle button is the minimal change; restructuring the app for one mode switch is not warranted.

lineage.spawnReason: The tree view established collapsible lineage threads; the graph shows full topology at a glance.

implementation: FeatureGraph.tsx uses D3 force simulation (forceLink + forceManyBody + forceCenter + forceCollide) rendered into an SVG. Nodes sized 5–14px by completenessOf(f) — computed at render time from optional-field coverage (analysis, implementation, decisions, successCriteria, knownLimitations, tags, domain). colorBy prop ('status' | 'domain') drives node colour and legend: status mode uses warm palette constants (STATUS_NODE_COLOR / STATUS_GLOW); domain mode maps 14 known domains to evenly-spaced HSL hues via domainColor/domainGlow helpers. When colorBy=domain, cluster forces (forceX/Y strength=0.18) pull nodes toward pre-computed domain centre positions arranged on a circle at 28% of canvas radius; cluster labels render in a dedicated SVG layer. Hover shows a floating tooltip; click pins a full info panel showing featureKey, title, status, domain, and problem text — click the × or click background to dismiss. Ctrl+click calls onNodeJump(featureKey) which switches the app to tree view and scrolls to the node. Clicking a node also calls centerOnNode which animates a D3 zoom/pan transition to bring the node to viewport centre at scale 1.8×. simulationRef and zoomRef persist across renders so focus changes and colorBy changes don't rebuild the simulation. focusKey change triggers centerOnNode if simulation is at rest, or queues a pendingCenterRef for the 'end' event (with a 700ms timeout fallback). The graph receives filteredFeatures from App so filter/search state is reflected in graph view. Expand-all and collapse-all buttons are shown in tree view only.
successCriteria: Graph toggle shows a force-directed graph where: nodes are sized by completeness, draggable, and highlight adjacent edges on hover; clicking a node pins an info panel and pans the graph to centre on it; colorBy toggle switches between status palette and domain-clustered palette with cluster labels; Ctrl+click opens the node in tree view; the graph respects active filter state; mobile-width screens show a notice instead of the graph.
knownLimitations: No ResizeObserver — graph doesn't reflow on window resize without page reload
features.find() is O(n) per D3 hover event — should be a pre-built Map for performance at scale
Pinned panel position is fixed relative to click coordinates — can be clipped on small canvases
Cluster centre positions are computed once at simulation build time; adding/removing domains requires a rebuild

statusHistory.reason: Graph view significantly enhanced: domain coloring (colorBy prop, cluster forces, cluster labels), pinned tooltip panel showing problem text, Ctrl+click to jump to tree view (onNodeJump), centerOnNode pan/zoom on click, faster simulation (alphaDecay=0.08), filter integration (graph now shows filteredFeatures), domain legend, resolved knownLimitation about completeness_pct

annotations.author: lac advance

revisions.reason: Graph view substantially enhanced: domain coloring, cluster forces/labels, pinned panel, onNodeJump, centerOnNode, filter integration, faster simulation. Removed resolved knownLimitation about completeness_pct.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Analysis was never updated after reopen — it still described the original minimal graph. Updated to reflect domain coloring, colorBy prop, cluster forces/labels, pinned panel, Ctrl+click jump, centerOnNode, and filter integration. Also removed the stale-review annotation since all stale fields are now resolved.
